source_text: In TLS 1.3, the TLS server indicates its version using the "supported_versions" extension (Section 4.2.1), and the legacy_version field MUST be set to 0x0303, which is the version number for TLS 1.2.
rule_statement: The server MUST set the legacy_version field in ServerHello to 0x0303 (TLS 1.2) when negotiating TLS 1.3.
applies_when: Server is constructing a ServerHello for TLS 1.3
checkability_rationale: Direct value comparison on a fixed field in ServerHello construction.

source_text: The last 8 bytes MUST be overwritten as described below if negotiating TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.1, but the remaining bytes MUST be random.
rule_statement: If the server negotiates TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.1, the last 8 bytes of the ServerHello random field MUST be overwritten with the downgrade sentinel value.
applies_when: Server negotiates TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.1 in response to a ClientHello
expected_value: last 8 bytes set to downgrade sentinel
checkability_rationale: Byte comparison of last 8 bytes in the random field against known sentinel values.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with clear conditions and values specified.

source_text: The last 8 bytes MUST be overwritten as described below if negotiating TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.1, but the remaining bytes MUST be random.
rule_statement: The first 24 bytes of the ServerHello random field MUST be generated by a secure random number generator (must be random).
applies_when: Server is generating the ServerHello random field
checkability_rationale: Verifying randomness requires statistical tests; cannot be checked by code inspection alone.

source_text: This structure is generated by the server and MUST be generated independently of the ClientHello.random.
rule_statement: The server MUST generate the ServerHello random value independently of the ClientHello.random value.
checkability_rationale: Can verify that server random generation code does not use ClientHello.random as input, but proving independence statistically is harder.

source_text: A client which receives a legacy_session_id_echo field that does not match what it sent in the ClientHello MUST abort the handshake with an "illegal_parameter" alert.
rule_statement: A client MUST abort the handshake with an illegal_parameter alert if the ServerHello legacy_session_id_echo does not match the legacy_session_id sent in ClientHello.
checkability_rationale: Direct byte comparison between sent and received session ID values.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with specific alert and clear condition.

source_text: A client which receives a cipher suite that was not offered MUST abort the handshake with an "illegal_parameter" alert.
rule_statement: A client MUST abort the handshake with an illegal_parameter alert if the ServerHello cipher_suite was not in the ClientHello.cipher_suites list.
applies_when: Client receives ServerHello with a cipher_suite value
expected_value: must be a value from ClientHello.cipher_suites
checkability_rationale: Direct membership check of server-selected cipher suite against client-offered list.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with clear condition and alert.

source_text: legacy_compression_method: A single byte which MUST have the value 0.
rule_statement: The legacy_compression_method field in ServerHello MUST have the value 0.

source_text: The ServerHello MUST only include extensions which are required to establish the cryptographic context and negotiate the protocol version.
rule_statement: The ServerHello MUST only include extensions required to establish cryptographic context and negotiate protocol version (supported_versions, pre_shared_key, key_share).
checkability_rationale: Can enumerate extensions in ServerHello and verify each is in the allowed set.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with enumerated allowed extensions.

source_text: All TLS 1.3 ServerHello messages MUST contain the "supported_versions" extension.
rule_statement: All TLS 1.3 ServerHello messages MUST contain the supported_versions extension.
checkability_rationale: Direct check for presence of supported_versions extension in ServerHello.

source_text: Upon receiving a message with type server_hello, implementations MUST first examine the Random value and, if it matches this value, process it as described in Section 4.1.4).
rule_statement: Upon receiving a server_hello message, implementations MUST first examine the Random value and if it matches the SHA-256 of "HelloRetryRequest", process it as a HelloRetryRequest per Section 4.1.4.
applies_when: Client receives a message with type server_hello
checkability_rationale: Can verify that parsing code checks the random field against the known sentinel before further processing.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with defined comparison value.

source_text: TLS 1.3 servers which negotiate TLS 1.2 or below in response to a ClientHello MUST set the last 8 bytes of their Random value specially in their ServerHello.
rule_statement: TLS 1.3 servers negotiating TLS 1.2 or below MUST set the last 8 bytes of their ServerHello Random value to the appropriate downgrade sentinel.
applies_when: TLS 1.3-capable server negotiates TLS 1.2 or below
checkability_rationale: Direct byte comparison of last 8 bytes against known constants.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with exact byte values provided.

source_text: If negotiating TLS 1.2, TLS 1.3 servers MUST set the last 8 bytes of their Random value to the bytes: 44 4F 57 4E 47 52 44 01
rule_statement: When negotiating TLS 1.2, a TLS 1.3 server MUST set the last 8 bytes of ServerHello.Random to 0x444F574E47524401.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with exact hex bytes.

source_text: If negotiating TLS 1.1 or below, TLS 1.3 servers MUST, and TLS 1.2 servers SHOULD, set the last 8 bytes of their ServerHello.Random value to the bytes: 44 4F 57 4E 47 52 44 00
rule_statement: When negotiating TLS 1.1 or below, a TLS 1.3 server MUST set the last 8 bytes of ServerHello.Random to 0x444F574E47524400.
applies_when: TLS 1.3 server negotiates TLS 1.1 or below
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with exact hex bytes.

source_text: TLS 1.3 clients receiving a ServerHello indicating TLS 1.2 or below MUST check that the last 8 bytes are not equal to either of these values.
rule_statement: TLS 1.3 clients receiving a ServerHello indicating TLS 1.2 or below MUST check that the last 8 bytes of Random are not equal to either downgrade sentinel value.
applies_when: TLS 1.3 client receives ServerHello indicating TLS 1.2 or below
expected_value: last 8 bytes must NOT equal 0x444F574E47524401 or 0x444F574E47524400
checkability_rationale: Direct byte comparison of last 8 bytes against two known constants.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with clear condition and specific alert on match.

source_text: If a match is found, the client MUST abort the handshake with an "illegal_parameter" alert.
rule_statement: If the client detects a downgrade sentinel match in the ServerHello Random field, it MUST abort the handshake with an illegal_parameter alert.
applies_when: Client finds downgrade sentinel in ServerHello.Random last 8 bytes when negotiating TLS 1.2 or below
checkability_rationale: Can verify that code aborts with correct alert when sentinel is detected.

source_text: A legacy TLS client performing renegotiation with TLS 1.2 or prior and which receives a TLS 1.3 ServerHello during renegotiation MUST abort the handshake with a "protocol_version" alert.
rule_statement: A legacy TLS client performing renegotiation with TLS 1.2 or prior that receives a TLS 1.3 ServerHello during renegotiation MUST abort the handshake with a protocol_version alert.
applies_when: Legacy client performing renegotiation receives TLS 1.3 ServerHello
checkability_rationale: Requires renegotiation code path to be present and handling the TLS 1.3 version check.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with specific condition and alert.
